market_sentiment.label: 中性
market_sentiment.summary: 資料更新中，請稍後再試

articles.title: White House says it received no Democratic nominees for SEC, CFTC vacancies
articles.description: Both US financial regulators are understaffed at the leadership level and lacking Democratic commissioners, with no announcement from President Donald Trump on filling the roles.

articles.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
articles.description: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

articles.title: Phantom, Hyperliquid ask CFTC to modernize rules for onchain derivatives
articles.description: The companies urged the regulator to exempt blockchain developers and non-custodial wallet providers from rules designed for traditional financial intermediaries.

articles.title: Newest version of crypto Clarity Act may drop as soon as next week, sources say
articles.description: After a long stretch without breakthroughs in the crypto market structure bill, insiders say a new draft may emerge in a final push for late-July action — though it still lacks bipartisan buy-in.

articles.title: Charles Hudson shares the common mistakes he’s seen after investing in 500+ startups
articles.description: In this week’s episode of Build Mode,  Isabelle Johannessen talks with Precursor Ventures' Charles Hudson about the headwinds facing early-stage founders today and the most common mistakes founders should avoid in order to get funded.

articles.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
articles.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

articles.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
articles.description: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

articles.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
articles.description: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

articles.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
articles.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

articles.title: Billions flowing out of bitcoin ETFs and private credit funds suggest rising market risks
articles.description: Redemption requests in the $2 trillion private credit market surged to $15.6 billion in the second quarter, dwarfing bitcoin ETF outflows.

articles.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
articles.description: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

articles.title: Bitcoin miners’ AI pivot faces investor scrutiny over insider sales
articles.description: As AI-driven mining stocks retreat, investors are examining executive stock sales, governance and shareholder alignment across leading Bitcoin miners, according to Blocksbridge Consulting.

articles.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
articles.description: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

articles.title: UK politicians mull permanent crypto donation ban in wake of Nigel Farage scandal
articles.description: Labour lawmakers proposed a full moratorium on crypto donations in March, which some have sought be made permanent after millions of dollars in contributions were linked to Reform leader Nigel Farage.

articles.title: Can Bitcoin hold $62K ahead of Friday’s $1.4 billion options expiry?
articles.description: US 10-year Treasury yields nears dangerous level while major Deribit Bitcoin options expiry approaches.

articles.title: MARA shares surge after 2 GW Texas infrastructure deal expands AI ambitions
articles.description: The Bitcoin miner's shares climbed about 15% after announcing it's acquiring a Texas site with up to 2 GW of power capacity to expand its AI and digital infrastructure strategy.

articles.title: Bitcoin traders reveal key levels as BTC price passes $63K after Trump Iran 'deal' comments
articles.description: Bitcoin gained new upside targets for the daily close and beyond after US president Donald Trump said that Iran "wants to make a deal."

articles.title: AI contracts, not bitcoin, now drive miner valuations, and Cipher and TeraWulf look cheap, says analyst
articles.description: Compass Point analysts Michael Donovan and Ed Engel said markets are giving little credit to future AI data center pipelines despite billions of dollars in signed leases.

articles.title: Block reaches $45M settlement with 46 states over Cash App fraud probe
articles.description: State attorneys general said they found that Block misled users by falsely advertising that Cash App provided bank-like protections, including advanced fraud detection.
